mechanism_narrative: Low and stable unemployment indicates a healthy labor market, supporting consumer confidence and spending, which translates to positive corporate earnings and S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. This reflects a period of economic stability.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 40% of periods where UNRATE is below 8.5% over any 3-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is at historically low levels, it signals an accommodative monetary policy, reducing borrowing costs for corporations and stimulating investment, leading to higher equity valuations and positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-stat < 1.5) when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% over a 5-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production combined with robust retail sales indicates healthy consumer and business demand, suggesting economic expansion and higher corporate profits, which drives S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as investors anticipate improved earnings.
failure_condition: If the correlation between the combined signal (INDPRO and RSAFS growth) and 20-day forward returns is negative or statistically insignificant (p > 0.1) over any 3-year period, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in housing starts during a post-recession recovery period indicates renewed consumer confidence and investment in real estate, which has positive spillover effects on the broader economy and equity markets, leading to positive 60-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If the average 60-day forward return is not positive when HOUST is above 600k units during identified recovery periods, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Stable and low inflation, as indicated by the CPI, provides a predictable economic environment, reducing uncertainty for businesses and investors. This stability supports equity valuations and positive S&P 500 returns over a short 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of periods where CPIAUCSL 12-month change is between 1% and 3%, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in non-farm payrolls signifies a strengthening labor market, leading to increased consumer spending and corporate revenues. This positively impacts S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as economic activity accelerates.
failure_condition: If the average 20-day forward return is negative when PAYEMS 3-month average change exceeds 100k over any 4-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating GDP growth coupled with declining unemployment rates indicates a robust economic expansion, signaling strong corporate earnings potential and investor confidence. This leads to positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon as the economy strengthens.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth > 2.5% AND UNRATE 3-month change < -0.2pp
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are not positive in at least 60% of instances where both conditions are met over any 5-year period, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Weak retail sales during periods of high inflation suggest eroding consumer purchasing power, which negatively impacts corporate revenues and profit margins. This leads to lower S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as economic headwinds intensify.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances during periods where CPIAUCSL 12-month change is above 4%, the hypothesis is rejected.
